tweets.title: The Indian Army's Army Uniforms-2026 manual overhauls military dress codes by shedding colonial-era vestiges and adopting indigenous elements. Key updates include introducing formal bandi jackets, making parade swords optional, creating unified winter and combat uniform categories, and strictly regulating grooming standards.
tweets.content: The Indian Army's Army Uniforms-2026 manual overhauls military dress codes by shedding colonial-era vestiges and adopting indigenous elements. Key updates include introducing formal bandi jackets, making parade swords optional, creating unified winter and combat uniform categories, and strictly regulating grooming standards.

tweets.title: RT by @OsintTV: I hope the Indian Police Forces also make necessary modifications of their colonial practices in their uniforms and customs/traditions. Hon'ble HM @AmitShah ji for information.
tweets.content:
I hope the Indian Police Forces also make necessary modifications of their colonial practices in their uniforms and customs/traditions. Hon'ble HM @AmitShah ji for information.

OsintTV 📺 (@OsintTV)

🇮🇳 Breaking from Colonial Legacy, Indian Army Embraces Indian Identity.

📍The Indian Army has unveiled a new dress policy aimed at phasing out several colonial era traditions and symbols.

📍Senior officers will wear Indian-style bandi jackets at official functions, replacing the traditional lounge suit.

📍Reviewing Officers will no longer carry ceremonial swords during parades and inspections.

📍The ceremonial sword presentation tradition at commissioning ceremonies is being discontinued.

📍The "Royal" designation and other British-era references are being gradually removed from military traditions and institutions.

📍Officers have been instructed to avoid unnecessary display of medals, badges and ceremonial accessories during routine functions.

📍Uniformity in dress standards has been introduced across commands to promote a modern and professional appearance.

📍Visible tattoos and body piercings have been restricted in line with the new policy.

📍The Army has prescribed guidelines for hairstyles, grooming and appearance to maintain uniform standards.

📍Colonial customs associated with dress and ceremonial practices are being phased out.

📍The reforms are part of the Army's broader effort to decolonise military traditions and strengthen a distinctly Indian identity.

From bandi jackets to the end of ceremonial swords and royal tags, the Indian Army is turning the page on its colonial past and embracing its own traditions.

— https://nitter.net/OsintTV/status/2066150841057210792#m
tweets.author: @spvaid

tweets.title:
🇮🇳 Breaking from Colonial Legacy, Indian Army Embraces Indian Identity.

📍The Indian Army has unveiled a new dress policy aimed at phasing out several colonial era traditions and symbols.

📍Senior officers will wear Indian-style bandi jackets at official functions, replacing the traditional lounge suit.

📍Reviewing Officers will no longer carry ceremonial swords during parades and inspections.

📍The ceremonial sword presentation tradition at commissioning ceremonies is being discontinued.

📍The "Royal" designation and other British-era references are being gradually removed from military traditions and institutions.

📍Officers have been instructed to avoid unnecessary display of medals, badges and ceremonial accessories during routine functions.

📍Uniformity in dress standards has been introduced across commands to promote a modern and professional appearance.

📍Visible tattoos and body piercings have been restricted in line with the new policy.

📍The Army has prescribed guidelines for hairstyles, grooming and appearance to maintain uniform standards.

📍Colonial customs associated with dress and ceremonial practices are being phased out.

📍The reforms are part of the Army's broader effort to decolonise military traditions and strengthen a distinctly Indian identity.

From bandi jackets to the end of ceremonial swords and royal tags, the Indian Army is turning the page on its colonial past and embracing its own traditions.

📍Uniformity in dress standards has been introduced across commands to promote a modern and professional appearance.

📍The reforms are part of the Army's broader effort to decolonise military traditions and strengthen a distinctly Indian identity.

BREAKING: British forces have boarded a sanctioned Russian "shadow fleet" oil tanker in the English Channel for the first time.
Royal Marine Commandos, intercepted and searched the vessel Smyrtos during a six-hour operation.

tweets.title:
🚨 From Battlefield Command to Military Transformation: Lt Gen Dhiraj Seth Set to Lead the Indian Army

📍Lt Gen Dhiraj Seth will take over as the next Chief of Army Staff on June 30.

📍He has commanded two operational Army Commands, South Western Command and Southern Command, a rare distinction among Indian Army officers.

📍Played a key role in driving force modernisation and integrating emerging technologies into the Army's future warfighting doctrine.

📍Brings nearly 40 years of military experience across conventional and counter insurgency operations.

📍Has commanded the prestigious Sudarshan Chakra Corps, an Armoured Brigade and a Counter Insurgency Force in J&K.

📍Currently serves as the Vice Chief of Army Staff.

📍An NDA alumnus, he was commissioned into the Armoured Corps in 1986.

📍Has undergone advanced military training in India and France, including the National Defence College.

India's next Army Chief takes charge at a time when the Indian military is rapidly transforming to meet future challenges

India's next Army Chief takes charge at a time when the Indian military is rapidly transforming to meet future challenges

Women, children, the elderly and the youth continue to gather at the protest site, sending a clear message to the authorities that years of oppression and repression are no longer acceptable.

Ministry of Defence, Government of India (@SpokespersonMoD)

The Government has appointed Lieutenant General Dhiraj Seth, PVSM, UYSM, AVSM, presently serving as the Vice Chief of the Army Staff, as the next Chief of the Army Staff in the substantive rank of General with effect from the afternoon of 30 June 2026.

An alumnus of the National Defence Academy and an Armoured Corps officer, Lt Gen Seth brings with him nearly four decades of distinguished service, marked by extensive command, staff and strategic appointments across diverse operational and institutional domains.

He will assume office upon the retirement of General Upendra Dwivedi on 30 June 2026.

Read here: pib.gov.in/PressReleasePage.…

Two things India should closely pay attention to with a clear understanding of what needs to be taught throughout the national security apparatus of this nation.

First : 
An intelligence report prompted the Indian government to put Starlink on hold as SpaceX deployed Starlink terminals in the war in West Asia.

An interesting thing is that Starlink doesn't have an operational license in Iran to provide satellite service.

Later, we saw the US banning Fable and Mythos from being utilised by any "non-US national" because they have jurisdiction over US nationals, and they can put them into misery if they try to do naughty stuff that those models are capable of.

CONCLUSION: Any American company, or as a matter of fact, any country's business that is rising superfast, especially in global markets, has the absolute backing of the American war machinery. 

And they follow no rules if it comes to their interest, and rightly so.

Their rise is fast for a reason, kindly plan your policy accordingly.

tweets.title: RT by @elitepredatorss: We should import chat gpt pro (Rafale/Su57) as stop gap.
tweets.content:
We should import chat gpt pro (Rafale/Su57) as stop gap.

Sridhar Vembu (@svembu)

This is big: all access to Mythos and Fable AI models disabled for everyone outside America.

First thoughts:

1. Technology is the ultimate weapon. National sovereignty, national security, all of it is now about technology.

2. Globalization is dead and Bharat must find her own way ahead.

We must keep these two ideas in mind.

What can our government do right now? Ensure that orgs in India embrace smaller models, both Indian and Chinese open source ones. With a bit of effort, we can make them work. Anyway, why pay money to people who don't even want to sell to you?

We must deepen our R&D. Sarvam has been on it and we have been on it but remember that the latest models cost not only huge GPU budgets to train, the GPUs themselves are restricted. So we can't afford the scale of money (of the order of $100+ billion to even get in the game!) and even if we could come up with the money, we can't get all the GPUs. I would not like to ask the government to fund tens of billions of dollars on this anyway - the money has far better uses.

Zoho has been pursuing alternative R&D approaches that are far, far less expensive but by its nature cutting edge R&D takes time and we are patient. I am confident we will get there.

Any remaining people in India who have delusions about globalization should wake up now.

— https://nitter.net/svembu/status/2065637118999990528#m
tweets.author: @alpha_defense

tweets.title:
Meghalaya has successfully completed approximately 90% of the border fencing along its international boundary with Bangladesh.

Total Border Length: 443 km
Fencing Completed: 400 km (90%)
Pending Stretch: 40–45 km

The remaining 10% of the project is delayed due to specific logistical and local grievances. In Meghalaya, unlike other states, many villages and farmlands are situated right near the zero line. Constructing a fence 150 yards away from the zero line would split villages and disrupt subsistence agriculture. The state government is currently engaging with local communities to resolve these alignment issues.
tweets.content:

tweets.title:
BREAKING: India Demonstrates Multi-Layered Ballistic Missile Defence Capability

The DRDO has successfully conducted three consecutive flight tests, demonstrating a multi-layered defence shield against long-range ballistic missile threats and validating a new medium-range naval anti-ship missile.

Defence Minister Rajnath Singh said the tests place India among an elite group of nations capable of intercepting ballistic missiles, including threats in the ICBM class.

The trials successfully validated multiple interceptors and advanced technologies designed to counter evolving missile threats.

The maiden flight test of the Naval Anti-Ship Missile (Medium Range) was also successfully completed, marking another milestone for India's indigenous defence capabilities.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: India Demonstrates Multi-Layered Ballistic Missile Defence Capability

The DRDO has successfully conducted three consecutive flight tests, demonstrating a multi-layered defence shield against long-range ballistic missile threats and validating a new medium-range naval anti-ship missile.

Defence Minister Rajnath Singh said the tests place India among an elite group of nations capable of intercepting ballistic missiles, including threats in the ICBM class.

The trials successfully validated multiple interceptors and advanced technologies designed to counter evolving missile threats.

The maiden flight test of the Naval Anti-Ship Missile (Medium Range) was also successfully completed, marking another milestone for India's indigenous defence capabilities.

tweets.title:
India's Missile Shield Gets Stronger 🇮🇳

India has joined an elite group of nations with the successful testing of key Ballistic Missile Defence and anti ship missile technologies.

During the trials conducted by DRDO on June 10 and 11, the indigenous Phase II BMD system successfully intercepted high speed ballistic targets representing threats with ranges of up to 5,000 km. The tests also validated a long range anti ship missile capability designed to engage hostile naval assets at extended distances.

These achievements mark another major step towards a robust, multi layer defence architecture built on indigenous technology.

The message is clear.

India can protect. India can strike.

tweets.title:
3 consecutive flight-tests were conducted on June 10 & 11, 2026 by the #DRDO to demonstrate multi-layered defence against long range BMs (interceptor AD-1 and AD2) and anti-ship capability at medium range(#NASM MR). 
BM targets range: 2000km to 5000kms+ interception capability
tweets.content:
3 consecutive flight-tests were conducted on June 10 & 11, 2026 by the #DRDO to demonstrate multi-layered defence against long range BMs (interceptor AD-1 and AD2) and anti-ship capability at medium range(#NASM MR).
BM targets range: 2000km to 5000kms+ interception capability
tweets.author: @DefenceDecode

tweets.title:
RT by @notam_in: The @DRDO_India has successfully demonstrated multiple crucial technologies bolstering nations defence capabilities against different types of enemy threats. 

Three consecutive flight-tests were successfully conducted to demonstrate multi-layered defence against long range Ballistic Missiles and Anti-ship capability at medium range.

Multi-layered BMD capability was successfully demonstrated. The interceptors successfully engaged their respective targets. The systems are designed and developed with latest technologies to address the emerging missile threats.

These tests have put India in elite group of nations having Ballistics Missile Defence Capabilities to engage Ballistic Missiles up to ICBMs. Anti-ship defence at medium range was demonstrated during the maiden flight test of Naval Anti-Ship Missile-Medium Range. Congratulations to DRDO on successfully demonstrating these crucial technologies.
tweets.content:
The @DRDO_India has successfully demonstrated multiple crucial technologies bolstering nations defence capabilities against different types of enemy threats.

Three consecutive flight-tests were successfully conducted to demonstrate multi-layered defence against long range Ballistic Missiles and Anti-ship capability at medium range.

These tests have put India in elite group of nations having Ballistics Missile Defence Capabilities to engage Ballistic Missiles up to ICBMs. Anti-ship defence at medium range was demonstrated during the maiden flight test of Naval Anti-Ship Missile-Medium Range. Congratulations to DRDO on successfully demonstrating these crucial technologies.

tweets.title:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
12th Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief
 
● The IDF carried out precision
strikes on Hezbollah infrastructure
in southern Lebanon, particularly around Bayt al-Siyad and Bint Jbeil.

● The Foreign Ministers of Iran and France discussed the latest regional developments, diplomatic efforts mediated by Pakistan, and bilateral issues.

● Leaders of Qatar, Pakistan, and the UAE called President Trump, urging him to refrain from striking Iran and stating that a deal with Tehran was within reach.

● According to Iranian sources, the proposed MoU with the US includes lifting sanctions, ending the naval blockade, and the withdrawal of US forces around Iran.

● US forces shot down two Iranian attack drones targeting commercial shipping, hours after President Trump called off planned strikes on Iran.

● As of today, CENTCOM has redirected 136 commercial vessels and disabled 9 to enforce compliance with the naval blockade on Iranian ports.

END OF THE BRIEF
tweets.content:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
12th Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● The IDF carried out precision
strikes on Hezbollah infrastructure
in southern Lebanon, particularly around Bayt al-Siyad and Bint Jbeil.

● The Foreign Ministers of Iran and France discussed the latest regional developments, diplomatic efforts mediated by Pakistan, and bilateral issues.

● Leaders of Qatar, Pakistan, and the UAE called President Trump, urging him to refrain from striking Iran and stating that a deal with Tehran was within reach.

● According to Iranian sources, the proposed MoU with the US includes lifting sanctions, ending the naval blockade, and the withdrawal of US forces around Iran.

● US forces shot down two Iranian attack drones targeting commercial shipping, hours after President Trump called off planned strikes on Iran.

● As of today, CENTCOM has redirected 136 commercial vessels and disabled 9 to enforce compliance with the naval blockade on Iranian ports.

END OF THE BRIEF

Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
11th Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● Bahraini air defences intercepted Iranian drones over Hamad Town and Manama. Debris struck homes and vehicles, injuring an 11 year-old girl, according to the Interior Ministry.

● Iran launched heavy waves of missiles and attack drones targeting Ali Al Salem and Ahmad Al-Jaber airbases in Kuwait, which host key US military detachments.

● India confirmed that three Indian sailors were killed in yesterday’s US strike on a tanker in the Gulf of Oman. The vessel had been accused of violating the naval blockade.

● The Indian-crewed tanker MT Jalveer suffered damage near Oman. All 20 Indian nationals onboard are reported safe.

● Iran’s Persian Gulf Strait Authority (PGSA) announced the complete closure of the Strait of Hormuz. Holders of existing transit permits have been asked to wait until further notice.

● As of today, CENTCOM has disabled 9 non-compliant vessels, redirected 135 ships that complied, and allowed 42 vessels supporting humanitarian aid to pass.

END OF THE BRIEF

— https://nitter.net/NetramDefence/status/2065109498323468372#m

tweets.title:
RT by @OsintTV: Main maut hoon , mera intezar kar, Gar teri nazar mere madar e watan par.

Main khauf hoon , mera zikr na kar, Gar tere dil mein hai Khuda ka dar.

Main rooh-e-sukoon hoon, main hifazat ka naam hoon,
Har be gunah ki amanat, har watan parast ka paigham hoon.

Jo mere mulk ki taraf badhe buri nazar ke saath, Uske liye main bijli hoon, uske liye main qahar hoon.

Aur jo mere madar-e-watan ki fikr dil mein rakhta hai,
Uske liye main aman hoon, uske liye main aitbaar hoon.

SOG J&K Police
